headline: Block, Atlassian, Meta Have Cut 20,000+ Jobs—What Do Prediction Markets Say About Unemployment?
summary: Salesforce Inc CEO Marc Benioff said last week that warnings about AI-driven mass layoffs were overblown. The numbers say otherwise. Block Inc cut 4,000 jobs in February, roughly 40% of staff, with CEO Jack Dorsey explicitly blaming AI. Atlassian Corporation followed with 1,600 layoffs last week to “self-fund” its AI pivot. Meta Platforms Inc is now reportedly planning to cut over 15,000 workers, or 20% of its workforce, to offset AI infrastructure spending that could hit $135 billion this year.

Meta Platforms Inc is now reportedly planning to cut over 15,000 workers, or 20% of its workforce, to offset AI infrastructure spending that could hit $135 billion this year.

headline: Square Report Finds Group of Loyal ‘Regulars’ Generates 6x More Revenue for Canada’s Small Businesses
summary: TORONTO, March 18, 2026--A small but loyal group of repeat customers generates nearly six times more annual revenue for Canadian neighbourhood businesses than one-time visitors, according to new data released today by technology company Square.

headline: XYZ stock has given up its prior gain. Block shares were trading higher after Truist Securities upgraded its rating on the stock from Hold to Buy and raised its price target from $72 to $77. Also, Rothschild & Co upgraded the stock from Sell to Neutral and raised its price target from $45 to $55.

headline: Square's 2026 Local Economy Report Reveals the Neighborhood Network Effect: Regular Customers Drive 6X More Revenue and Create Interconnected Local Economies
summary: SAN FRANCISCO, March 18, 2026--Square today published its 2026 Local Economy Report report, a first-of-its-kind analysis revealing how "regulars" and the neighborhood networks they create are the backbone of thriving local economies. The report reveals that regular customers drive 6x more revenue for local businesses and unpacks the patterns and trends in their spending.
